'''Richard Agar''' (born 20 January 1972) is an English professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the former [[head coach]] of the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] and a former professional rugby league footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s.

He played at club level for the [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] ([[Dewsbury Rams|Rams]]) (two spells), [[Featherstone Rovers]] (two spells), the [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Rochdale Hornets]], and has coached at representative level for [[France national rugby league team|France]], and at club level for the [[York City Knights]], [[Hull F.C.]] (two spells, initially as Assistant Coach), the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], and [[Warrington Wolves]] (First Team Coach {{circa|2014…2015}}).<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background ==
Richard Agar's birth was registered in [[Pontefract]] district, [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England. He is the son of the rugby league footballer and coach; [[Allan Agar]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Agar played for [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Widnes Vikings]], where he played 16 games in 2001 and won a [[Rugby Football League Championship Second Division|Northern Ford Premiership]] Grand Final winners medal, but he turned down the opportunity to play for Widnes Vikings in 2002's [[Super League VII]].

Agar made his début for [[Featherstone Rovers]] on Sunday 16 June 2002, and he played his last match for Featherstone Rovers during the 2003 season.

==Coaching career==
He spent four years as assistant coach at [[Hull F.C.]] under [[John Kear]] and [[Peter Sharp (rugby league)|Peter Sharp]]. In 2008 [[Hull F.C.]] appointed Richard Agar as their Head Coach following the departure of Sharp.<ref name="Coaches and Captains">{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/club/history-stats/past-coaches-captains|title=Coaches and Captains|publisher=hullfc.com|date=31 December 2016|access-date=1 January 2017}}</ref>

In September 2011 it was announced that Agar had signed a three-year deal with [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], and would become their head coach following the end of the 2011 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14855723|title=Wakefield Wildcats appoint Hull FC's Richard Agar|date=9 September 2011|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=9 September 2011}}</ref>

In February 2013, it was announced that Agar would coach [[France national rugby league team|France]] for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/8507851/france-appoint-wakefield-boss-richard-agar-as-new-head-coach|title= France appoint Wakefield boss Richard Agar as new head coach|work=Sky Sports|date=18 February 2013|access-date=3 June 2014}}</ref> Agar coached [[France national rugby league team|France]] to the World Cup Quarter-Final

On 2 June 2014, Agar quit his job with Wakefield Trinity Wildcats with immediate effect and was replaced by his assistant [[James Webster (rugby league)|James Webster]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30356/agar-replaced-by-webster|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606231522/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30356/agar-replaced-by-webster|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 June 2014|title=Agar replaced by Webster|publisher=Superleague.co.uk|access-date=3 June 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/9335474/super-league-richard-agar-steps-down-as-wakefield-coach|title=Super League: Richard Agar steps down as Wakefield coach|work=Sky Sports|date=2 June 2014|access-date=3 June 2014}}</ref>

On 10 June 2014, it was confirmed Agar would become first team coach of [[Warrington Wolves]] for the [[Super League XX|2015 Super League season]] working alongside [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27785262|title=Warrington Wolves: Richard Agar to join Tony Smith's staff|date=10 June 2014|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=10 June 2014}}</ref>

On 29 August 2016, Agar resigned as head coach of the French national team. It is believed he left because new Fédération Française de Rugby à XIII president [[Marc Palanques]] wanted a Frenchman to coach the national team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_23333-agar-departs-france-job.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160915170242/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_23333-agar-departs-france-job.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=15 September 2016|title=Agar departs France job|publisher=LOVERUGBYLEAGUE|access-date=29 August 2016}}</ref>

On 17 October 2020, he coached Leeds in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Salford at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 November 2020}}</ref>
In the [[2021 Super League season]], he guided Leeds to a fifth-placed finish on the table as the club qualified for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com|accessdate=27 September 2021}}</ref>

On 21 March 2022, Agar announced he would step down as [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] head coach with immediate effect.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60821849|title=Richard Agar: Leeds Rhinos head coach steps down from role|date=21 March 2022|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=21 March 2022}}</ref>

On 3 August 2022, Agar was announced as assistant coach for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] on a two-year deal commencing in 2023. He would join head coach [[Andrew Webster (rugby league)|Andrew Webster]], his fellow [[Samoa national rugby league team|Toa Samoa]] assistant coach and younger brother his former assistant [[James Webster (rugby league)|James]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2022/08/03/agar-onboard-as-assistant-coach/|title=Experienced Agar signed as assistant coach from 2023|accessdate=13 October 2022}}</ref>
